_meta.purpose: Stale-CLI-subprocess reaper tunables. Loaded by HarnessCLIConductor at fire time — every value here is hot-reloadable via refreshMchataisourceCache (zero rebuild). Reaps standalone PTY sessions that have been silent (no stream activity) longer than the threshold. Pinned (wizard-attached) sessions are NEVER reaped.
_meta.tuning_advice: Default 60min idle gives a long-running generation 1h of headroom before the reaper considers it stuck. Bump higher for users running multi-hour autonomous CLI sessions; drop lower for tighter retail behavior. NEVER set < 5min — short-burst silence between tool calls is normal and you'd kill live sessions.

_doc_enabled: Master kill-switch. When false, the reaper is a no-op and stale processes accumulate. Useful as an emergency disable.
_doc_idleThresholdSeconds: A session must have NO stream activity (no output, no input) for at least this many seconds AND be unpinned to be reaped. 3600 = 60 min. Prior incident 2026-04-26: 9-day-old Codex zombie + 3-day-old Gemini zombies were running because nothing kills them.
_doc_reapOnLaunch: Run the reaper once on app launch (after ShellBridge is ready). Catches zombies that survived a previous app crash or normal-but-unclean exit.
_doc_reapOnForceRelease: Run the reaper when the user clicks Force-Release. The yellow triangle is the user saying 'something is wrong, clean up' — reap stale standalones in the same gesture.
_doc_minIdleSecondsForLaunchReap: On launch, allow a lower threshold (5 min) since any session left over from a prior run is suspect. Force-Release uses the full idleThresholdSeconds so we don't accidentally kill a foreground user's long generation.

pgrepPatterns.label: Claude Code (CLI)
pgrepPatterns._doc: VSCode-extension and homebrew Claude Code subprocesses run with these flags. Spawned by mChatAI+ via WizardCLIRunner OR by other tools (VSCode Anthropic extension); the conductor only tracks the former.

pgrepPatterns.label: Gemini CLI
pgrepPatterns._doc: Homebrew gemini binary. Same shape as Claude Code — subprocess outlives parent on timeout/crash.

pgrepPatterns.label: Codex CLI
pgrepPatterns._doc: Codex JSON-mode subprocesses. NOTE: 'codex app-server' is the OpenAI ChatGPT desktop app's runtime — explicitly NOT in this pattern so we don't kill the user's IDE. Pattern requires --output-format json which is wizard-spawned only.

_doc_pgrepPatterns: Process-name patterns the broader reaper checks via pgrep -af. Each pattern matches the FULL command line (so 'codex.*--output-format json' matches the wizard-spawned variant but not 'codex app-server'). Patterns are regex-style. Add new entries when a new CLI backend is wired into WizardCLIRunner. To disable pgrep reaping entirely: set pgrepPatterns: [].
_doc_pgrepReapThresholdSeconds: Process-age threshold for the pgrep reaper. Default 600 = 10min. Conservative — we don't want to interrupt a real wizard generation, and the conductor-side reaper (idleThresholdSeconds=3600) already handles the long-tail cases. The pgrep reaper is the safety net for processes the conductor doesn't see at all (e.g. VSCode-extension Claude Code).
_doc_pgrepProtectCurrentSession: When true, never kill a process that shares the current Claude Code session ID (--resume <uuid>). Without this, a Claude Code agent running inside a Claude Code agent would suicide on launch reap. Reads $CLAUDE_PROJECT_KEY or matching --resume token to identify self.
